title: REDI-NET W-1 WATER FIELD SAMPLING
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3en41","text":"RISKS AND PERSONAL PROTECTION","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8206j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f351e","text":"Know the risk associated with the study site and sampling location and take appropriate safety precautions and/or wear appropriate equipment.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fv63","text":"Wear light-colored long pants and a long sleeve shirt. Wear rubber boots or hiking shoes and white socks, and tuck pant legs into socks. Be sure that clothing is sturdy and can withstand long thorns.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dumpq","text":"Never open a sample container until the instant you will take the sample.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85b40","text":"Samples should be collected WITHOUT ENTERING THE WATER, i.e. the collector stands only on the waterhole bank or muddy pond edges. This prevents disturbance of the substrate and may limit cross-contamination.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: NOTE : Suggested sampling frequency at each site is one time per month or, when not logistically possible, at minimum two times per unique season representative of the laboratory setting (e.g., early wet/late wet; early dry/late dry; rainy/dry season). Field sampling of eDNA water samples involves two people. One person serves as the ‘sampler’ and the other person serves as a ‘helper’. The helper can look up details in these instructions when needed, keep track of samples, handle objects that are contamination risks, serve as a second set of eyes for potential contamination, and ensure safety of the sampler in potentially hazardous field conditions.
Find a suitable sampling location within the water body representative of the ecological systems, if possible.

Water samples should be taken from the edge of the waterhole at the same sample location of soil collection. Water collection should be done in triplicate (3x 250 mL ) if the water is clear but collect only one sample ( 250 mL ) if the water is cloudy.
Because ‘the edge’ of the water body may change due to precipitation changes, the edge should be defined at each month's sampling.

If the water body has a "drop off" (embankment of more than 20 cm ), the soil and water samples should be taken where the water "meets" the soil. Aim to collect at locations where finer sediment is present, if possible.

NOTES: Water samples collection should be performed before sediment samples collection (REDI-NET SOP S-1 Soil Collection). The helper opens the bag containing the vials to have one open and ready for the sampler. The helper will open and close the bags containing the collection materials. This is to prevent potential contamination of samples.

The Sampler takes the appropriately labeled water dipper from the bag and opens the corresponding Whirl-Pak® Bag which can be placed in a bucket for easier manipulation. NOTE : A single dipper can be used for all triplicate water samples from the same sampling location (for example, edge replicate 1, 2, and 3).
Check the depth of the water. If the depth of the water is greater than 1 meter, the water column should be gently mixed using the water dipper without disturbing bottom sediment.

Once the water column is homogenized, collect water filling the dipper, and empty the sample into the Whirl-Pak® Bag until the 250 mL sample is collected (3/4 of a 532 mL Whirl-Pak® Bag). NOTE : Remove debris from water samples as much as possible before pouring into Whirl-Pak® bags.
Once the water is collected, measure pH of the sample by emerging litmus paper.
Close the Whirl-Pak® Bag and dry it using paper towels.
Label the Whirl-Pak® Bag with a Sharpie or water-proof marker.
Place the Whirl-Pak® Bags into the cooler box filled with ice for storage.
Proceed with REDI-NET SOP S-1 Soil Collection at the same sampling location.
Repeat the water and soil sampling 3 times per sampling location point.

When all samples for a collection site are taken, bring the samples back to the laboratory facility for further processing according to REDI-NET SOP W-2 Water Processing (filtration should be done within 24:00:00 ).
Keep freshly collected water in labeled whirl-pak bags at 4 °C cold chain (cooler box) until return to laboratory. Water samples can be stored at 4 °C for up to 1 week.

Once at the laboratory, it is optimal to filter the water samples and freeze the filter disk in separate, labeled ziplock bags as soon as possible. If water samples can not be processed on the same day of collection, whole sample bags should be frozen at -80 °C . If -80 °C storage is unavailable, water bags/ filter disks should be stored at -20 °C until processing. Water samples can be stored at -20 °C for up to 1 month and at -80 °C for longer term storage.
creator: REDI-NET Consortium
